laws: Law 3 — no secret tokens under frontend/
Law 6 — no sweep anims · quranlyai.com · no raw brand hex · 3 fonts
Law 2 — every tenant-table migration enables RLS

assertions: signup auto-creates a public.profiles row
profile email mirrors auth.users
profile name pulled from signup metadata
profile avatar pulled from signup metadata
B cannot SELECT A's auth_events
B CAN SELECT its own auth_events
B cannot directly INSERT an auth_event (no client insert policy)
append-only: B cannot UPDATE its own auth_events
B cannot SELECT A's profile (self-only)
create_workspace provisions an agency + the caller's OWNER membership
provisioning enqueues a queued workspace.provision job (defaults deferred)
sub-account created under the agency (parent set)
agency admin canNOT read a sub-account without an explicit membership (RLS §1)
B cannot SELECT/UPDATE A's workspace, memberships, or invitations
B (non-admin) cannot create an invitation in A
accept_invitation redeems a token → membership with the invited role
transfer_ownership swaps owner↔admin and updates owner_id
guard_last_owner blocks orphaning a workspace; leave blocked for sole owner
archive/restore toggle status; only the owner may archive
member can insert only status='queued' jobs (not 'running')
5 built-in roles seeded; permission arrays match the registry (no drift)
STAFF blocked from crm.delete and crm.export server-side (has_permission=false)
OWNER short-circuits to all; ADMIN lacks billing.manage / workspace.delete
MANAGER has crm.delete but not team.manage
CLIENT is portal-only; coarse RLS blocks a client write regardless of toggles
per-member overrides read server-side: grant adds, revoke wins
built-in roles immutable — cannot update, delete, or forge one
custom role clone → assign (role syncs to base_role) → toggle → delete guard
set_member_role refuses owner tier + protects the sole owner + rejects non-admin
cross-tenant: B cannot select/insert/update A's roles or change A's members
has_permission_for (service path) mirrors has_permission for an explicit user
meter_increment appends a usage_events ledger row AND upserts usage_meters atomically
ledger row stores a real unit_cost (rebilling data, §8)
N increments accumulate to exactly N (row-lock upsert; no lost updates)
credit wallet is drawn on increment and clamps at 0 (never negative)
meter_check returns included/used/remaining and over=true past quota+wallet
has_feature reads plan feature_gates (agency has m34_voice; free does not; unknown→false)
trial-expiry sweep flips a lapsed trialing workspace to billing_state='trial_expired'
cross-tenant: B cannot SELECT A's subscription/usage_meters/usage_events/credit_wallets
stripe_events is invisible to authenticated (service-role only)
usage_meters writes are service-role only (client cannot INSERT/UPDATE directly)
plans is a global catalog readable by any authenticated user (positive control)
null-aware uniqueness: one workspace override + one platform default per provider
scope/workspace_id CHECK: scope='platform' iff workspace_id is null
SELECT is admin+ on workspace rows; manager/staff/client see none (§2)
cross-tenant: B's admin cannot SELECT/write A's integrations
platform (null) rows are visible only to a platform-admin (is_platform_admin)
writes are service-role only: admin cannot insert/update/delete directly (D-029)
service role (worker) can update health columns (bypasses RLS)
resolveCredential order: workspace override beats platform default; else default; else none
integration.health_check is queued-only from the client (RLS)
hourly cron enqueues one health job per connected workspace integration
provider registry: _shared/providers.ts and js/providers.js agree (no drift, 20 providers)
B cannot SELECT workspace A
B cannot SELECT A's memberships
B cannot SELECT A's jobs
B cannot UPDATE workspace A
B cannot INSERT a job into workspace A
B cannot INSERT a non-queued job (queued-only enforced)
B CAN SELECT its own workspace B
A staff CAN INSERT a queued job into workspace A
claim_job: queued → running
claim sets lease (locked_by/locked_at)
claim increments attempts
worker: running → done (with done_at)
claim_job on empty queue returns nothing
B cannot SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE A's workflows/versions/executions/steps
B CAN read global templates (positive control)
staff read · manager+ write · client cannot even read (internal-ops ceiling)
executions + steps are system-written (no client INSERT policy — Gate-4)
emit_trigger enrols matches + enqueues an automation.execute job
trigger_config {tag_id} narrows: wrong tag does not enrol, right tag does
re-entry: once blocks re-enrol · allow re-enrols · once_per_days:7 blocks inside window
contact insert → contact.created; deal move → deal.stage_changed; inbound msg → message.received
automation_apply_move_deal does NOT re-emit (loop guard) + logs deal_change via=automation
version pinning: editing nodes bumps version + snapshots; old snapshot unchanged (AC-3)
templates: global read · own-workspace save · cannot insert a global one
walker paused at WAIT; delayed resume job enqueued (run_after future); resume pinned to successor
add_tag wrote a real contact_tags row (tag created on demand)
IF/ELSE lead_score>0 → TRUE branch; second contact (0) → FALSE branch
internal_notification called the real notify() RPC (1 feed row)
resume walked to GOAL → completed; ≥5 step rows logged (green/red timeline)
completed execution idempotent; test-mode send_email suppressed (Gate-3: nothing metered)
media_folders + media_assets RLS-on; assets carry sel/ins/upd/del policies
cross-tenant: B cannot SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE A's folders or assets
bucket role matrix: media=staff write/manager delete · brand=admin · client ceiling
register_media_asset records the index row + enqueues one queued media.autotag job for images
non-image → tag_status='skipped' + no autotag job; foreign-workspace path rejected
used_in: register appends + dedups; a second distinct usage appends; unregister removes
backfill_asset_usage populates used_in {pipeline, deal_id} from deal_files (idempotent)
recompute_storage_meter writes storage_gb = Σbytes/GB as a GAUGE (re-run overwrites, not adds)
soft_delete_asset (manager+) hides the asset from the browse SELECT; the row survives (recoverable)
blog_articles/article_revisions/article_categories/article_authors RLS-on; 4 policies on blog_articles
cross-tenant: B cannot SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE A's articles, categories, or authors
role matrix: staff create/edit · manager+ delete/publish/approve/reject · client CEILING (cannot even read)
article_revisions is append-only (client cannot INSERT directly — definer RPC owns the write)
save_article_revision snapshots + prunes to the last 20; restore reverts content as a draft
publish_article flips status + stamps published_at + builds Article JSON-LD (manager+ only)
schedule_article + publish_due_articles publishes DUE rows, skips future ones (the cron body)
editorial: submit_for_review → in_review; approve → published; reject stores feedback + returns to draft
(site_id, slug) is unique per site; the same slug on a different site is allowed
blog index lists given (published) articles as cards + canonical + RSS link + brand vars
single article: stored Article JSON-LD + meta_title/desc + canonical + og:image + body + byline
empty schema falls back to a minimal Article node; category page labels + canonical
RSS 2.0 well-formed with one item per article; not-found shell is 404 + noindex
content-seo scoreArticle: 0..100, deterministic, checklist states pass/warn/fail, keyword-in-title pass
empty article → score 0 no throw; missing keyword → warn not throw; Flesch positive/empty=0
plainText strips script + tags (no code leaks into word count/readability)
content-editor sanitizeHtml: strips on* handlers, <script>, javascript: href; keeps a[href,title]/img[src,alt]/semantic blocks; unwraps disallowed tags keeping text
